ontology.foodex2_description: Cereal-based sweet snack — glutinous rice and sesame confection
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for sesame roll; A0CKP (cereal-based sweet snack) is the closest defensible anchor. Exact code should be confirmed against EFSA FoodEx2 catalogue. Changed match_type to 'none' as 'approximate' is not a valid option.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept identified for sesame roll confection.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical sesame roll piece weighs approximately 40 g and is commonly consumed as a snack or light dessert in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Sesame Roll — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Sesame Roll (cr_id F11090055). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glutinous rice-based confection — analogous estimate based on glutinous rice GI data (GI ~65–72) from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Singaporean sesame roll in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 based on glutinous rice (GI ~65–72, University of Sydney GI Database) as the primary carbohydrate base. The high sugar content (26.1 g/100g) and malt component may elevate effective GI; conservative mid-range estimate of 65 applied. GL per serving = (65 × 41.4 × 0.40) / 100 = 10.8 g per 40 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar load (26.1 g/100g) driving insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 85 mg/100g is moderate but the sugar burden is the primary concern
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit sesame roll to no more than 30 g on rare occasions, as the high sugar content promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and may elevate blood pressure indirectly despite the moderate sodium level.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 26.1 g/100g and carbohydrates at 41.4 g/100g with a medium-high estimated GI (~65) produce a significant postprandial glucose spike
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sesame roll is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high sugar content (26.1 g/100g) and high carbohydrate load (41.4 g/100g); if consumed at all, limit to a single small piece (20–25 g) as an infrequent treat and monitor blood glucose response closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat at 29.3 g/100g with estimated saturated fat ~4.1 g/100g; sesame seeds contribute beneficial MUFA and PUFA but the high sugar and energy density (478 kcal/100g) pose cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume sesame roll sparingly (≤30 g per occasion) given its high energy density and fat content; the sesame seed component provides some cardioprotective unsaturated fats, but the high sugar load and overall caloric density outweigh this benefit at typical serving sizes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 26.1 g/100g exceeds the 15 g/100g RA threshold, promoting pro-inflammatory mediators; deep-fried preparation generates AGEs that amplify joint inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit sesame roll due to its very high sugar content (26.1 g/100g) which promotes systemic inflammation; baked preparations are preferable to deep-fried, but the sugar burden remains the dominant concern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sesame Roll (芝麻卷; Gulung Bijan) is a traditional Singaporean local ethnic snack made from glutinous rice, malt, sugar, and sesame seeds. The confection is formed by rolling a sweetened glutinous rice mixture into a cylindrical shape and coating it generously with toasted sesame seeds, which are pressed into the surface to adhere. The texture is characteristically chewy and dense from the glutinous rice base, with a nutty crunch from the sesame seed coating and a pronounced sweetness from the malt and sugar components. The flavour profile combines the earthy nuttiness of sesame with the caramel-like sweetness of malt. Nutritionally, sesame roll is energy-dense at 478 kcal per 100 g, with a high fat content of 29.3 g/100g (largely from sesame seeds, which are rich in monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fatty acids), a high sugar content of 26.1 g/100g, and a moderate protein content of 12.2 g/100g contributed by the sesame seeds. The sodium content is relatively low at 85 mg/100g. Clinically, the very high sugar content makes this snack unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing blood glucose levels. The high energy density and fat content require moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease. The sesame seeds contribute beneficial minerals including calcium (63 mg/100g) and iron (4.2 mg/100g), as well as dietary fibre (3.6 g/100g). Sesame roll is commonly sold at traditional confectionery stalls and wet markets across Singapore and is associated with festive and everyday snacking occasions.
common_user_queries: What is sesame roll made of?
How many calories in a sesame roll?
What is the GI of sesame roll?
Is sesame roll suitable for hypertension?
What are the ingredients in sesame roll?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Sesame Roll (cr_id F11090055) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. The food identity, macronutrient profile, and micronutrient data are all from this approved primary source. GI is an analogous estimate based on glutinous rice data from the University of Sydney GI Database, which reduces GI confidence to medium for that specific field.
notes: Saturated fat estimated at 4.1 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central sesame seed fatty acid profile applied proportionally to the fat content reported by HPB; the HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this item. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food item and no supplementary approved-source data was available to complete them. GI value of 65 is an analogous estimate based on glutinous rice GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Singaporean sesame roll was found in any approved GI source. Dietary tags from input retained: plant_based, low_sodium, high_protein, high_fibre, high_fat_warning, high_sugar_warning. Note that 'high_protein' tag reflects 12.2 g/100g from sesame seeds; this is above average for a snack but should be interpreted in context of typical portion size (~40 g). Halal status assumed based on plant-based ingredients; formal halal certification not confirmed.
